You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the mbed-os codebase.
ARM Mbed OS is an embedded operating system for ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers. It includes a hardware abstraction layer (HAL), RTOS primitives, peripheral drivers (SPI, I2C, UART, CAN, USB), connectivity (BLE, Wi-Fi, cellular), storage, and security. C/C++ codebase.

TOOL USAGE — you MUST use these exact formats:

To run a shell command:
<bash>your_command_here</bash>

To view a file:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>

To view specific lines:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor> [start_line, end_line]

To edit a file (replace text):
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>exact text to replace</old_str>
<new_str>replacement text</new_str>

When finished, say: SUBMIT

IMPORTANT: Each response must contain EXACTLY ONE tool call. Do NOT put multiple tool calls in one response.

Working directory is /repo.
You will be given a PR description. Implement the described changes.

Please implement the following pull request:

## Title: Fix Copy-Paste Error in PDM-PCM Interrupt Documentation

### Summary
This PR corrects a documentation copy-paste error in the `Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt` function within the PDM-PCM driver header file. The function's description incorrectly stated that it "sets an INTR register's bits" when it actually clears interrupt statuses by writing to the INTR register. This inconsistency likely arose from duplication of documentation from a similar function without proper adaptation.

The fix ensures that the documentation accurately reflects the function's behavior, improving code clarity and maintainability for developers working with the PDM-PCM driver on Cypress PSoC 6 platforms.

### Why
- **Accuracy**: Ensures documentation matches actual implementation
- **Maintainability**: Reduces confusion for developers using or modifying the driver
- **Code Quality**: Fixes a clear copy-paste artifact that could mislead future development

### Changes
- `targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h`: Updated the function documentation for `Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt` to correctly describe its behavior of clearing interrupt statuses by writing to the INTR register instead of incorrectly stating it sets bits.

### Testing
No functional changes were made; this is a documentation-only fix. The change was verified by reviewing the surrounding interrupt-related functions to ensure consistency and correctness of the updated description.

1 /***************************************************************************//** 2 * \file cy_pdm_pcm.h 3 * \version 2.30 4 * 5 * The header file of the PDM_PCM driver. 6 * 7 ******************************************************************************** 8 * \copyright 9 * Copyright 2016-2020 Cypress Semiconductor Corporation 10 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 11 * 12 * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); 13 * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License. 14 * You may obtain a copy of the License at 15 * 16 * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 17 * 18 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software 19 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, 20 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. 21 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and 22 * limitations under the License. 23 *******************************************************************************/ 24 25 /** 26 * \addtogroup group_pdm_pcm 27 * \{ 28 * 29 * The pulse-density modulation to pulse-code modulation (PDM-PCM) driver provides an 30 * API to manage PDM-PCM conversion. A PDM-PCM converter is used 31 * to convert 1-bit digital audio streaming data to PCM data. 32 * 33 * The functions and other declarations used in this driver are in cy_pdm_pcm.h. 34 * You can include cy_pdl.h to get access to all functions 35 * and declarations in the PDL. 36 * 37 * Features: 38 * * Supports FIFO buffer for Incoming Data 39 * * Supports Software Mute Mode 40 * * Programmable Gain Settings 41 * * Programmable Word Length 42 * 43 * Pulse-density modulation, or PDM, represents 44 * an analog signal with a binary signal. In a PDM signal, specific amplitude values 45 * are not encoded into codewords of pulses of different weight as they would be 46 * in pulse-code modulation (PCM); rather, the relative density of the pulses corresponds 47 * to the analog signal's amplitude. The output of a 1-bit DAC is the same 48 * as the PDM encoding of the signal. 49 * 50 * Pulse-code modulation (PCM) is the method used to digitally represent sampled analog signals. 51 * It is the standard form of digital audio in computers, compact discs, digital telephony, 52 * and other digital audio applications. In a PCM stream, the amplitude of the analog signal 53 * is sampled regularly at uniform intervals, and each sample is quantized 54 * to the nearest value within a range of digital steps. 55 * 56 * \section group_pdm_pcm_configuration_considerations Configuration Considerations 57 * 58 * To set up a PDM-PCM, provide the configuration parameters in the 59 * \ref cy_stc_pdm_pcm_config_t structure. 60 * 61 * For example, set dataStreamingEnable to true, configure rxFifoTriggerLevel, 62 * dmaTriggerEnable (depending on whether DMA is going to be used), 63 * provide clock settings (clkDiv, mclkDiv and ckoDiv), set sincDecRate 64 * to the appropriate decimation rate, wordLen, and wordBitExtension. 65 * No other parameters are necessary for this example. 66 * 67 * To initialize the PDM-PCM block, call the \ref Cy_PDM_PCM_Init function, providing the 68 * filled \ref cy_stc_pdm_pcm_config_t structure. 69 * 70 * If you use a DMA, the DMA channel should be previously configured. PDM-PCM interrupts 71 * (if applicable) can be enabled by calling \ref Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterruptMask. 72 * 73 * For example, if the trigger interrupt is used during operation, the ISR 74 * should call the \ref Cy_PDM_PCM_ReadFifo as many times as required for your 75 * FIFO payload. Then call \ref Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt with appropriate parameters. 76 * 77 * ... [truncated] ... 2.20.1 100 * Snippet updated. 101 * Old snippet outdated. 102 * 103 * 104 * 2.20 105 * Flattened the organization of the driver source code into the single 106 * source directory and the single include directory. 107 * 108 * Driver library directory-structure simplification. 109 * 110 * 111 * Added register access layer. Use register access macros instead 112 * of direct register access using dereferenced pointers. 113 * Makes register access device-independent, so that the PDL does 114 * not need to be recompiled for each supported part number. 115 * 116 * 117 * 2.10 118 * The gain values in range +4.5...+10.5dB (5 items) of /ref cy_en_pdm_pcm_gain_t are corrected. 119 * Added Low Power Callback section. 120 * Incorrect setting of gain values in limited range. 121 * Documentation update and clarification. 122 * 123 * 124 * 2.0 125 * Enumeration types for gain and soft mute cycles are added.
126 * Function parameter checks are added.
127 * The next functions are removed: 128 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_EnterLowPowerCallback 129 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_ExitLowPowerCallback 130 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_EnableDataStream 131 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_DisableDataStream 132 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_SetFifoLevel 133 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_GetFifoLevel 134 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_EnableDmaRequest 135 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_DisableDmaRequest 136 * 137 * The next functions behaviour are modified: 138 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_Enable 139 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_Disable 140 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterruptMask 141 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_GetInterruptMask 142 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_GetInterruptStatusMasked 143 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_GetInterruptStatus 144 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt 145 * * Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterrupt 146 * 147 * The Cy_PDM_PCM_GetFifoNumWords function is renamed to Cy_PDM_PCM_GetNumInFifo.
148 * The Cy_PDM_PCM_GetCurrentState function is added. 149 * 150 * Improvements based on usability feedbacks.
151 * API is reworked for consistency within the PDL. 152 * 153 * 154 * 155 * 1.0 156 * Initial version 157 * 158 * 159 * 160 * 161 * \defgroup group_pdm_pcm_macros Macros 162 * \defgroup group_pdm_pcm_functions Functions 163 * \{ 164 * \defgroup group_pdm_pcm_functions_syspm_callback Low Power Callback 165 * \} 166 * \defgroup group_pdm_pcm_data_structures Data Structures 167 * \defgroup group_pdm_pcm_enums Enumerated Types 168 * 169 */ 170 171 #if !defined(CY_PDM_PCM_H__) 172 #define CY_PDM_PCM_H__ 173 174 /******************************************************************************/ 175 /* Include files */ 176 /******************************************************************************/ 177 178 #include "cy_device.h" 179 180 #if defined (CY_IP_MXPDM) 181 182 #include "cy_pdm_pcm_v2.h" 183 184 #endif /* CY_IP_MXPDM */ 185 186 #if defined (CY_IP_MXAUDIOSS) 187 188 #include "cy_syslib.h" 189 #include "cy_syspm.h" 190 #include 191 #include 192 193 #ifdef __cplusplus 194 extern "C" 195 { 196 #endif 197 198 /****************************************************************************** 199 * Global definitions 200 ******************************************************************************/

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [200, 300]

Let me continue searching for the function documentation:

Now let me find the specific documentation for the `Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt` function:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [700, 800]

Let me search for the interrupt-related functions documentation earlier in the file:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [500, 600]

Let me continue searching for the GetInterruptStatus and ClearInterrupt functions:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_Cypress/TARGET_PSOC6/mtb-pdl-cat1/drivers/include/cy_pdm_pcm.h</str_replace_editor> [600, 700]

/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_GetInterruptStatus
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Reports the status of PDM-PCM interrupt sources (an INTR register).
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address.
* \return The interrupt bit mask \ref group_pdm_pcm_macros_interrupt_masks.
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE uint32_t Cy_PDM_PCM_GetInterruptStatus(PDM_Type const * base)
{
    return (PDM_PCM_INTR(base));
}


/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Clears one or more PDM-PCM interrupt statuses (sets an INTR register's bits).
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address
* \param interrupt
*  The interrupt bit mask \ref group_pdm_pcm_macros_interrupt_masks.
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE void Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt(PDM_Type * base, uint32_t interrupt)
{
    CY_ASSERT_L2(CY_PDM_PCM_IS_INTR_MASK_VALID(interrupt));
    PDM_PCM_INTR(base) = interrupt;
    (void) PDM_PCM_INTR(base);
}


/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterrupt
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Sets one or more interrupt source statuses (sets an INTR_SET register).
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address.
* \param interrupt
*  The interrupt bit mask \ref group_pdm_pcm_macros_interrupt_masks.
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE void Cy_PDM_PCM_SetInterrupt(PDM_Type * base, uint32_t interrupt)
{
    CY_ASSERT_L2(CY_PDM_PCM_IS_INTR_MASK_VALID(interrupt));
    PDM_PCM_INTR_SET(base) = interrupt;
}


/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_GetNumInFifo
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Reports the current number of used words in the output data FIFO.
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address.
* \return The current number of used FIFO words (range is 0 - 254).
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE uint8_t Cy_PDM_PCM_GetNumInFifo(PDM_Type const * base)
{
    return (uint8_t) (_FLD2VAL(PDM_RX_FIFO_STATUS_USED, PDM_PCM_RX_FIFO_STATUS(base)));
}


/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearFifo
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Resets the output data FIFO, removing all data words from the FIFO.
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address.
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE void Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearFifo(PDM_Type * base)
{
    PDM_PCM_RX_FIFO_CTL(base) |= PDM_RX_FIFO_CTL_CLEAR_Msk; /* clear FIFO and disable it */
    PDM_PCM_RX_FIFO_CTL(base) &= (uint32_t) ~PDM_RX_FIFO_CTL_CLEAR_Msk; /* enable FIFO */
}


/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_ReadFifo
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Reads ("pops") one word from the output data FIFO.
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address.
* \return The data word.
*
******************************************************************************/
__STATIC_INLINE uint32_t Cy_PDM_PCM_ReadFifo(PDM_Type const * base)
{
    return (PDM_PCM_RX_FIFO_RD(base));
}

* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Clears one or more PDM-PCM interrupt statuses (sets an INTR register's bits).
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address
* \param interrupt
*  The interrupt bit mask \ref group_pdm_pcm_macros_interrupt_masks.
*
******************************************************************************/</old_str>
<new_str>/******************************************************************************
* Function Name: Cy_PDM_PCM_ClearInterrupt
***************************************************************************//**
*
* Clears one or more PDM-PCM interrupt statuses (writes to the INTR register to clear).
*
* \param base The pointer to the PDM-PCM instance address
* \param interrupt
*  The interrupt bit mask \ref group_pdm_pcm_macros_interrupt_masks.
*
******************************************************************************/</new_str>
